message:
perf(search): lean filter-view parse for SemanticFilter hot path

SemanticFilter::matches runs once per HNSW-visited node during ANN
traversal — often thousands of times per query — and every call went
through parse_semantic_doc_id, which does a hex::decode_to_slice on
the 64-byte content_hash suffix. SemanticFilter never looks at
content_hash; the hex decode was pure waste on the critical path.

Added a parallel lean parser:

  pub(crate) struct SemanticDocIdFilterView {
      agent_id, workspace_id, source_id, role, created_at_ms
  }

  pub(crate) fn parse_semantic_doc_id_filter_view(doc_id: &str)
      -> Option<SemanticDocIdFilterView>

It shares the "m|" discriminator + splitn(8, '|') layout so field
positions stay byte-identical to the full parse, but stops parsing
after the created_at_ms field — skipping message_id, chunk_idx
(advanced-past with two `.next()?` calls to stay positional), and
the hex decode of content_hash entirely.

SemanticFilter::matches now calls the filter-view parser. A comment
at the call site explains the cost rationale so a future refactor
doesn't silently revert to the heavier parse. Both functions remain
in the same file and return Option so None propagates identically.

Full parse_semantic_doc_id is also cleaned up: the "m" discriminator
is now a single .strip_prefix("m|") instead of .split('|') + .next()
+ "m" compare, and the remaining field-extraction loop is capped
with splitn(8, '|') so parsers don't walk arbitrary tail '|' chars
that shouldn't exist in well-formed doc_ids.

Existing tests against parse_semantic_doc_id still apply unchanged;
a follow-up can add a byte-identical equivalence proof between the
two parsers on shared fields if we want that guarantee in CI.
